lede: A look at November 25 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Augusto Pinochet and Emperor Taizong of Liao, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Mirabal sisters
events.desc: The Mirabal sisters of the Dominican Republic are assassinated.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: After 42 days of fighting, the Battle of Triangle Hill ends in a Chinese victory. American and South Korean units abandon their attempt to capture the "Iron Triangle".

events.title: McCarthyism
events.desc: Red Scare: The "Hollywood Ten" are blacklisted by Hollywood movie studios.

events.title: Bosnia and Herzegovina
events.desc: World War II: Statehood of Bosnia and Herzegovina is re-established at the State Anti-fascist Council for the National Liberation of Bosnia and Herzegovina.

events.title: Berlin
events.desc: In Berlin, Germany and Japan sign the Anti-Comintern Pact, agreeing to consult on measures "to safeguard their common interests" in the case of an unprovoked attack by the Soviet Union against either nation.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: German forces defeat Portuguese army of about 1,200 at Negomano on the border of modern-day Mozambique and Tanzania.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Missionary Ridge: Union forces led by General Ulysses S. Grant break the Siege of Chattanooga by routing Confederate troops under General Braxton Bragg at Missionary Ridge in Tennessee.

events.title: 1759 Near East earthquakes
events.desc: An earthquake hits the Mediterranean destroying Beirut and Damascus and killing 30,000–40,000.

events.title: Trunajaya rebellion
events.desc: Trunajaya rebellion: After a long and logistically challenging march, the allied Mataram and Dutch troops successfully assault the rebel stronghold of Kediri.

events.title: Malcolm II of Scotland
events.desc: Máel Coluim mac Cináeda, King of Scots, dies. His grandson, Donnchad, son of Bethóc and Crínán of Dunkeld, inherits the throne.

births.name: Augusto Pinochet
births.line: Chilean general and politician, 30th President of Chile

births.name: Emperor Taizong of Liao
births.line: Liao dynasty emperor of China from 927 to 947

births.name: Pope John XXIII
births.line: Head of the Catholic Church from 1958 to 1963

births.name: Mauno Koivisto
births.line: Finnish banker and politician, 9th President of Finland

births.name: Carl Benz
births.line: German engineer and businessman, founded Mercedes-Benz

deaths.text: Fidel Castro, Communist leader of Cuba, and revolutionary
U Thant, Burmese lawyer and diplomat, 3rd Secretary-General of the United Nations
Malcolm II of Scotland, King of Alba from 1005 to 1034

observances.text: Christian feast day: Catherine of Alexandria and its related observances
Christian feast day: Isaac Watts (Lutheran Church and Church of England)
Christian feast day: James Otis Sargent Huntington (Episcopal Church)
International Day for the Elimination of Violence against Women
